title: 5 Enrolling in FX Drafts
body_text:
5	Enrolling in FX Drafts
5.1	FX Drafts Enrollment
Financial institutions that want to participate in FX Drafts must sign an agreement with Convera and Central 1. Central 1 provides the enrollment package that includes all necessary contracts and forms. Contact Treasury Services and Solutions to enroll in FX Drafts. See Section 1.3, Contacts, Websites, and Other Resources.
FX Drafts offers various setup options regarding settlement and reporting, rates, and authorization requirements. Early in the enrollment process, Convera provides a demonstration of FX Drafts and discusses product options with your financial institution.
After your financial institution returns the necessary forms and contracts, Central 1 assigns FX Drafts access to your User Management Security Officers (as requested by your financial institution). The User Management Security Officers can then assign FX Drafts access to other Client Centre Users.
FX Drafts Customization, Form 6299, is available on the Client Centre. You can use this form to notify Central 1 of changes after enrollment.
Prior to implementation, Convera will provide product training and an FX Drafts demo environment that reflects your financial institutionâs specifications.
Related Information
FX Drafts Customization,
Form 6299
5.2	FX Drafts Setup Considerations
FX Drafts facilitates various set-up options for settlement, reporting, rates, and authorization requirements. Complete FX Drafts Customization, Form 6299, to indicate your financial institutionâs preferences.

, for processing. Central 1 will forward the form to Convera for implementation.
